Lomatium alpinum, also known as Lomatium or Loxotrema, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lomatium include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and volatile oils, such as carvone and limonene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lomatium is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical use.
